title: Virgillian Civil War
content:
{{Top|fa|canon=Virgillian civil war}} {{Otheruses|Virgillia|more=Civil war}} {{War |prev= |conc=[[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]''[[The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook]]'' |next= |image= |name=Virgillian Civil War |begin=[[25 BBY/Legends|25 BBY]]{{HoloNetNewsWeb|45|jediwatch/13228_1.html|Virgillian Jedi Envoy Declared Lost}}, which establishes that the Virgillian Civil War began two [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior, is set on the [[Great ReSynchronization|date]] "13:2:28," which corresponds to [[23 BBY/Legends|23 BBY]] per the reasoning [[Template:YearMathGRS|here]]. Therefore the Virgillian Civil War must have started in [[25 BBY/Legends|25 BBY]]. |end=c. [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]According to ''[[The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook]]'', the Virgillian Civil War ended shortly before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' dates to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. |date= |place=[[Virgillia system/Legends|Virgillia system]] |result=[[Virgillian Free Alignment/Legends|Virgillian Free Alignment]] victory''[[The Truce at Bakura]]'' |battles= |side1=*Virgillian Free Alignment *[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] {{C|late conflict}} |side2=*[[Aristocracy]] *[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] {{C|no earlier than [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]}}''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' establishes that the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] was [[Declaration of a New Order|reorganized]] into the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. |side3= |side4= |commanders1=[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Tessa Manchisco]] |commanders2=Captain [[Jarroth]] |commanders3= |commanders4= }} {{Quote|Four Jedi—Masters Ludwin Katarkus and Everen Ettene plus their Padawans Danyawarra and Halagad Ventor—left Coruscant last month to try to settle the two-year old conflict between the Virgillian Free Alignment and the Aristocracy.|HoloNet News report|{{HoloNetNewsWeb|45|jediwatch/13228_1.html|Virgillian Jedi Envoy Declared Lost}}}} The '''Virgillian Civil War''' was an internal conflict fought between the revolutionary [[Virgillian Free Alignment/Legends|Virgillian Free Alignment]] and the ruling [[Aristocracy]] government of the [[Virgillia system/Legends|Virgillia system]] from [[25 BBY/Legends|25 BBY]] to approximately [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. Early in the war, an attempt by a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Envoy/Legends|envoy]] at mediation ended in the [[Death/Legends|death]] of half the envoy, and the conflict continued through the [[Declaration of a New Order|end]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and into the subsequent [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. The Free Alignment was severely constrained by its lack of [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] technology and [[Starship/Legends|starships]], but even when the Aristocracy aligned itself with the Empire and gained an Imperial [[Imperial Military/Legends|military]] presence in the [[Star system/Legends|system]], the Free Alignment was able to survive. Soon after the beginning of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] began supplying the Free Alignment with [[Weapon/Legends|weaponry]] and ships, which turned the Virgillian conflict into a proxy war between the Alliance and the Empire. A few [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] before the end of the Virgillian Civil War, the Alliance's delivery of eight [[Quasar Fire-class cruiser-carrier/Legends|''Quasar Fire''-class bulk cruisers]] turned the tide of the war, which, when combined with the Empire's withdrawal from the system as the Galactic Civil War intensified, resulted in a Free Alignment victory shortly before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. Virgillian [[Virgillian Free Alignment Starfleet|forces]] later fought with the Alliance at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. ==The war== ===Early conflict=== [[File:HalagadSkye.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Halagad Ventor survived an ill-fated attempt by a Jedi envoy to broker peace in the early Virgillian Civil War.]] The Virgillian Civil War broke out in [[25 BBY/Legends|25 BBY]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']]''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' [[Virgillia system/Legends|Virgillia system]]. It pitted the [[Aristocracy]], an autocratic government composed of [[Virgillian]] [[Noble/Legends|nobility]], against the rebel group known as the [[Virgillian Free Alignment/Legends|Virgillian Free Alignment]]. The Free Alignment consisted principally of non-nobles, and most of the combatants on both sides were [[Human/Legends|Humans]]. The Aristocracy confined Free Alignment activity to [[Colonist/Legends|colony]] [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] within the [[Star system/Legends|star system]]. One of those worlds, [[Virgillia 7]], was a hotbed of Free Alignment activity from the beginning of the war. In [[23 BBY/Legends|23 BBY]], a [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] team of two [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]], [[Ludwin Katarkus]] and [[Everen Ettene]], and their two [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]], [[Danyawarra]] and [[Halagad Ventor]], were [[Mission to Virgillia 7|sent]] to attempt to negotiate peace between the Aristocracy and the Free Alignment. However, the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Transport/Legends|transport]] was destroyed on arrival and it was believed that all four were [[Death/Legends|killed]], a fact that was reported in a [[HoloNet News CoCo District Edition|CoCo District Edition]] issue of the [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]] on the [[Great ReSynchronization|date]] 13:2:28.{{HoloNetNewsWeb|45|jediwatch/13228_1.html|Virgillian Jedi Envoy Declared Lost}} It was later discovered that Ventor''[[SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story]]'' and Danyawarra had, in fact, survived the attack.{{CSWECite|I|165|Danyawarra}} ===Imperial era=== At some point after the [[Declaration of a New Order|fall]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] and its reorganization into the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the Aristocracy aligned itself with Empire. During the Empire's [[Imperial Era/Legends|galactic reign]], the Aristocracy controlled all [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] technology in the Virgillia system, forcing the Alignment to fight Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] with primitive [[Weapon/Legends|armaments]] such as [[Virgillian wheel lock|flintlocks]] and [[Club/Legends|clubs]]. Nevertheless, despite such an imbalance in power, the Free Alignment still prevailed in several engagements. Imperial [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] at one point summarily executed the [[Parent/Legends|parents]] of the young Virgillian [[Tessa Manchisco]], who at the [[Time/Legends|time]] had been the leaders of the Free Alignment on Virgillia 7. When she grew up, Manchisco went on to help re-organize the Free Alignment from a group of [[Guerrilla warfare/Legends|guerrillas]] into a true military force. She also became one of the Free Alignment's first [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]], eventually gaining the rank of [[Captain/Legends|captain]]. ===Proxy war=== ====A noble defection==== [[File:Flurry_crew.png|thumb|left|250px|Tessa Manchisco, Monnda Tebbo, and Rannd Delckis on the deck of the ''Flurry'']] The [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] began supplying the Free Alignment with blasters and [[Starship/Legends|starships]] at some point after the beginning of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] in [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]]. The [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] patrolled the Virgillia system in search for any signs of either Free Alignment or Alliance operations,{{CSWECite|II|141|Jarroth, Captain}} with the [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Stormwind]]'' being one of the Imperial ships used for that purpose. Several [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] before the end of the war, an Imperial informant in the system alerted the ''Stormwind'' to a possible Free Alignment camp on the [[Virgillia's forested moon|forested moon]] of the planet [[Virgillia]]. The ''Stormwind''{{'s}} [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]], Captain [[Jarroth]], sent a landing party to investigate what was in fact a [[Unidentified refugee camp|refugee camp]]. Convinced the camp was being used as a hideout by the Free Alignment, stormtroopers subsequently [[The Burning/Legends|tortured]] and killed all of its inhabitants. The ''Stormwind'' was Imperial Navy [[Ensign/Legends|Ensign]] [[Rannd Delckis]]'s first posting, and his experience as part of the landing party horrified him so much that he defected to the Free Alignment soon afterward. As his parents were members of the Aristocracy, it was initially difficult for Delckis, who thus became the sole aristocratic member of the Free Alignment, to gain the trust of the rebels, but when he did not waver in the face of outright hostility from other Free Alignment members and proved himself to be highly competent in manipulating communications systems, he earned the respect of Manchisco and was allowed to serve as a [[Communications officer/Legends|communications officer]]. Delckis's Imperial training allowed him to both use and confuse Imperial [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] to great effect, and he was given two commendations for his work. ====The tide turns==== Weeks before the end of the war, the Alliance gave the Free Alignment eight [[Quasar Fire-class cruiser-carrier/Legends|''Quasar Fire''-class bulk cruisers]], accompanied by [[Chief Navigator]] [[Monnda Tebbo]]. Although the Free Alignment preferred to fight its own battles and had thus refused the Alliance offer of additional troops, the ''Quasar Fire''-class [[Bulk cruiser/Legends|bulk cruiser]] ''[[Flurry]]'' was considered such an unusual ship that Tebbo was sent to advise on its use. Tebbo also instructed the Free Alignment in conducting [[Space warfare/Legends|space warfare]], and those tactics were instrumental in the eventual Free Alignment victory. In gratitude, he was made an honorary Virgillian citizen and was also notable as one of the only non-Human combatants of the Virgillian Civil War. [[File:Flurryandx-wing.png|thumb|right|300px|The ''Flurry'' with a Rebel Alliance [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighter]]]] Throughout the war, the [[Virgillian Free Alignment Starfleet]] was small and, instead of firepower, relied on unconventional tactics to win engagements. As well as the eight bulk [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]] from the Alliance, the Alignment, as of the end of the civil war, had at least twenty [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] as well as [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunters]], [[Zebra starfighter]]s, and [[C-73 Tracker starfighter]]s. The ''Flurry'', under the command of Manchisco, served as the [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of the Virgillian [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], of which the ''[[Dukedoom]]'' was also a part. Near the end of the war, the Virgillians significantly upgraded their capital ships with captured Imperial technology, including weapons, [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]], sensors, and [[Computer/Legends|computers]] on four of their cruiser-[[Carrier/Legends|carriers]]. The ''Flurry'' also had a stolen [[HoloNet transceiver/Legends|HoloNet transceiver]], although it had a slow connection speed. As the Galactic Civil War intensified, the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] withdrew from the Virgillia system, which enabled the Free Alignment to achieve significant victories against the Aristocracy, including capturing several capital ships and technology it then used to upgrade its existing ships. The remaining Imperial vessels in the system, which were used by the Aristocracy, were outdated and, despite the Free Alignment fleet also largely consisting of antiquated ships, all Imperial ships were eventually either captured or destroyed in the last weeks of the war. Due to those victories, the Free Alignment was able to force the Aristocracy to agree to a truce shortly before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. ==Aftermath== {{Quote|Virgillia. Welcome. Do you need refueling? You've arrived at an opportune moment. We're about to jump.''"
"''Battle?''"
"''Confirmed. Big one...maybe our last, if we lose. We can use you.''"
"''We've got one wounded bird, but the rest of us are spoiling for a real fight. Top us off and transmit jump coordinates.''"
"''Splendid. Welcome to the Rebel Alliance.|A Rebel Alliance member and Tessa Manchisco, upon the arrival of the Virgillian task force near Sullust|{{WEGCite|story=Newest Rebels|book=The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook}}}} After an uneasy truce was established, the [[Virgillian Council]] was formed as the new government of the system. One of its first actions was to send a [[Task force/Legends|task force]] to join the [[Alliance Military/Legends|Alliance military]] near the planet [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]], where they were amassing in preparation to attack the Empire's [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] [[Death Star/Legends|superweapon]] at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. Although five of the Free Alignment's cruiser-carriers had been damaged to the point of being grounded and another one remained in the system in case of retaliatory Imperial attacks, the Virgillian Council was able to populate the task force with two cruiser-carriers, including the ''Flurry'', as well as twenty other combat capital ships. The task force went on to fight in the [[Bakura Incident]]. During that event, the ''Flurry'' was destroyed, with all hands, including the Virgillian Civil War combatants Manchisco, Delckis, Tebbo, and the pilot [[Valtis]], being lost. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:TruceatBakura.png|thumb|right|250px|The Virgillian Civil War was introduced in ''The Truce at Bakura''.]] The Virgillian Civil War was first mentioned in the [[1993]] novel ''[[The Truce at Bakura]]'' by [[Kathy Tyers]]. The [[1996]] ''[[The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook]]'', published by [[West End Games]] for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and set around the time of the Battle of Endor of 4 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]], states that the Rebel Alliance had been aiding the Virgillian Free Alignment in the civil war for at least seven [[Standard year/Legends|years]], meaning that the Alliance had been in existence since around [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]]. Since then, other sources, such as the [[2012]] reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' by [[Jason Fry]] and [[Paul R. Urquhart]], have overridden that fact by placing the formation of the Rebel Alliance in 2 BBY. In addition, [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' erroneously claims that the Virgillian Civil War lasted seven years,{{CSWECite|III|308|Virgillian Civil War}} despite [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 45|''HoloNet News Vol. 531 #45'']], a real-world [[HoloNet News (original website)|representation]] of an [[in-universe]] issue of the HoloNet News written by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] and [[Paul Ens]]{{HoloNetNewsWeb||credits.html|Credits}} and published before [[March 10]], [[2002]],{{JCFCite|url=threads/5990764|board=Literature|thread=The Official HoloNetNews Discussion Thread|user=IAmTheDarkSide|date=2002-03-10|time=7:24 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20201002154704/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-official-holonetnews-discussion-thread.5990764}} establishing that the conflict had already begun by 25 BBY. ==Appearances== *{{HoloNetNewsWeb|45|jediwatch/13228_1.html|Virgillian Jedi Envoy Declared Lost}} *''[[The Truce at Bakura]]'' {{1stm}}
